result.anchor_clause: Section 7.14 Specified IP Covenant, read together with Section 5.14 Designation and Section 5.15 Intellectual Property. Section 5.14 provides: "As of the Closing Date and at all times thereafter, each of the Co-Borrower, Holdings, Altuglas and Aristech are and shall remain an 'Unrestricted Subsidiary' of the Trinseo Lead Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries for purposes of, in accordance with and pursuant to the Prepetition OpCo Credit Agreement, the Prepetition Super-Priority Revolving Credit Agreement and any other Indebtedness of the Trinseo Lead Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries at any time outstanding, including without limitation, any refinancing Indebtedness of any of the foregoing." Section 5.15: "Trinseo Europe owns or otherwise has the right to license to Aristech and Altuglas, as applicable, the Specified IP Rights licensed under the applicable Specified IP License Agreement... Other than Trinseo Europe, Aristech and Altuglas, no other Loan Party owns, licenses or possesses the right to use any Specified IP Rights in North America."
result.admin_agent: Alter Domus (US) LLC, as Administrative Agent
result.collateral_agent: Alter Domus (US) LLC, as Collateral Agent
result.guarantors: The Guarantors party hereto from time to time (a class); if a Debtor, as Debtor and Debtor-in-Possession. Borrowers/Loan Parties include Trinseo PLC (Parent), Trinseo NA Finance LLC (Holdings), Trinseo Luxco Finance SPV S.à r.l. (Lead Borrower) and Trinseo NA Finance SPV LLC (Co-Borrower).

financial_covenants.threshold: Not stated in the provided text (Section 6.20 Liquidity and Section 7.17 Permitted Variance; Liquidity are referenced in the table of contents but the operative thresholds are not included in the excerpt)

financial_covenants.kind: Budget / permitted variance covenant (DIP budget and variance testing)
financial_covenants.threshold: Not stated in the provided text (Section 6.22 Budget and Variance Reporting and Section 7.17 Permitted Variance referenced in TOC; thresholds not in excerpt)
financial_covenants.test_frequency: Not stated in provided text (variance reporting)

financial_covenants.threshold: Not stated in the provided text (Section 6.23 Milestones referenced in TOC only)

baskets.name: Initial New Money Term Loans (Closing Date draw)
baskets.quote: on the Closing Date, in a single borrowing in an aggregate principal amount requested by the Lead Borrower not to exceed $35,000,000

baskets.name: Delayed Draw New Money Term Loans (upon Final DIP Order)
baskets.value: Up to the unfunded New Money Commitments as of such date
baskets.quote: upon entry of the Final DIP Order, in a single borrowing in an aggregate principal amount requested by the Lead Borrower not to exceed the unfunded New Money Commitments as of such date immediately prior to giving effect to such Borrowing

baskets.name: Roll-Up Term Loans (roll up of Prepetition Super HoldCo Secured Obligations)
baskets.value: Roll-up of prepetition Super HoldCo Secured Obligations per DIP Orders; roll only with Required Lenders' consent
baskets.quote: shall mean the 'roll up' of Prepetition Super HoldCo Secured Obligations into Roll-Up Term Loans pursuant to the terms of this Agreement and the DIP Orders

baskets.name: Permitted Investments (enumerated categories (a)-(hh))
baskets.value: Enumerated categories only (no general dollar-cap builder disclosed in excerpt); includes existing Investments, intercompany subordinated Investments, JV Interests ownership, cash/Cash Equivalents/Investment Grade Securities, deposits by Aristech/Altuglas to Trinseo Ireland Global IHB Limited, securitization Investments, etc.
baskets.quote: the Lead Borrower, in its sole discretion, will classify and may subsequently reclassify such Investment (or any portion thereof) in any one or more of the types of Investments described in clauses (a) through (hh) above

result.unrestricted_subsidiary_designation_flex: Highly constrained. Under Section 5.14, the Co-Borrower, Holdings, Altuglas and Aristech "are and shall remain an 'Unrestricted Subsidiary' of the Trinseo Lead Borrower and its Restricted Subsidiaries for purposes of... the Prepetition OpCo Credit Agreement, the Prepetition Super-Priority Revolving Credit Agreement and any other Indebtedness... at all times thereafter." This is a fixed designation within a DIP structure rather than a flexible re-designation mechanic; the excerpt does not disclose a general basket permitting new unrestricted-subsidiary designations by the Loan Parties.

lme_vectors.rationale: The agreement contains a dedicated Specified IP Covenant (Section 7.14) and represents that IP ownership/licensing is confined to identified entities (Trinseo Europe, Aristech, Altuglas), constraining transfer of material IP away from the credit group.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 7.14 Specified IP Covenant (TOC) plus Section 5.15 representation that only Trinseo Europe, Aristech and Altuglas own/license Specified IP Rights in North America, with a defined Specified IP License Agreement.
lme_vectors.quote: Other than Trinseo Europe, Aristech and Altuglas, no other Loan Party owns, licenses or possesses the right to use any Specified IP Rights in North America.

lme_vectors.rationale: New money is sized as fixed New Money Commitments (Initial $35,000,000 plus Delayed Draw up to unfunded commitments) with no reborrowing, but the operative Section 7.03 Indebtedness permitted-debt capacities are not contained in the provided excerpt.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 2.01 caps the Initial New Money Term Loans at $35,000,000 and Delayed Draw loans at unfunded New Money Commitments; Section 7.03 Indebtedness exists but its baskets are not in the excerpt.
lme_vectors.quote: In no event shall any Lender be required to make Term Loans in excess of its New Money Commitments. Once repaid or prepaid, the New Money Term Loans may not be reborrowed.

lme_vectors.rationale: This is a super-priority DIP facility with a Required Lenders construct that specially protects Designated Lenders holding >=75% of New Money, but the sacred-rights/voting amendment provisions governing priming are not in the provided text.
lme_vectors.basis: Required Lenders definition includes a Designated Lender protection and disregards certain Roll-Up Term Loans; Roll-Up occurs only with Required Lenders' consent.
lme_vectors.quote: Required Lenders shall include each Designated Lender for so long as such Designated Lender (together with its managed funds and accounts) holds at least 75% of the New Money Commitments and New Money Term Loans

lme_vectors.rationale: Section 7.06 Restricted Payments exists and Restricted Payments are broadly defined to include Restricted Investments, but the specific RP baskets and thresholds are not contained in the provided excerpt.
lme_vectors.basis: Section 7.06 Restricted Payments (TOC); Restricted Payment definition captures dividends, redemptions, returns of capital and any Restricted Investment; Permitted Investments are enumerated in clauses (a)-(hh).
lme_vectors.quote: any dividend or other distribution (whether in cash, securities or other property) with respect to any Equity Interest of any Loan Party... and (ii) any Restricted Investment.

Section 5.13Disclosure.
To the best knowledge of the Lead Borrower, no report, financial statement,
certificate or other written information furnished by or
on behalf of any Loan Party (other than projected financial information,
financial information and information of a
general economic or industry nature) to any Agent or any Lender in connection
with the transactions contemplated hereby and the negotiation
of this Agreement or delivered hereunder or any other Loan Document (as
modified or supplemented by other information so furnished) when
taken as a whole contains any material misstatement of fact or omits to state
any material fact necessary to make the statements therein
(when taken as a whole), in the light of the circumstances under which they
were made, not materially misleading. With respect to projected
financial information and financial information, the Borrowers represent that
such information was prepared in good
faith based upon assumptions believed to be reasonable at the time of
preparation of such materials; it being understood that such projections
may vary from actual results and that such variances may be material.
